injuryUpdates.injuryType: Omitted from 26-man squad (limited club minutes); named as reserve
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Reserve only — would need an injury call-up to feature at the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Limited playing time at club level all season; AFCON 2024 final hero left out of final 26

injuryUpdates.source: ESPN, BeIN Sports, Africa Soccer - 15 May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Although still an international, Haller was dropped from the main 26-man squad on 15 May 2026 and named only among the five reserves. Effectively unavailable for tournament selection barring a withdrawal. Use retired_international status as a 'not in squad' marker per scout convention.
Confirmed in 26-man squad. Singled out by coach and reporters as a standout attacking prospect for the tournament.

injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Available — included in 26-man squad as captain

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Forced off in the 32nd minute with a muscle injury; later reported as less serious than feared

injuryUpdates.source: AfricaSoccer, Foot Africa, GhanaSoccernet - late April 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Captain. Suffered a muscle injury against Al-Fateh that sparked concern in Abidjan. Reported as a minor muscular issue tied to fixture congestion. Selected in the 26-man squad on 15 May, remains the captain.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Knee injury sustained vs Fiorentina (Conference League, early April)
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned to Crystal Palace training in mid-May; selected in the 26-man squad

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Withdrawn with knee injury; missed nine Crystal Palace games before returning to training in mid-May

injuryUpdates.source: ESPN, Read Crystal Palace, RotoWire - 15 May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Coach Emerse Fae selected Guessand despite no club appearances since 9 April. Crystal Palace boss Glasner publicly confirmed return to training in mid-May. Will need a productive France friendly on 4 June to prove match fitness.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Strong Serie A season — 7 goals and 8 assists in 41 appearances across competitions

injuryUpdates.source: ESPN, BeIN Sports, World Soccer Talk - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: France youth international who completed an eligibility switch to Ivory Coast (approved 8 May 2026). Selected in the senior 26-man squad on 15 May despite being uncapped. Adds an in-form Inter Milan striker to compensate for Haller's omission.

tacticalUpdates.oldValue: Haller's hold-up play creating space for runners, with Pépé cutting inside from the right
tacticalUpdates.newValue: Pace-driven attack led by Amad Diallo and Simon Adingra in wide areas, with a rotating central focal point between Bonny, Wahi, Guessand and Pepe rather than Haller's hold-up play
tacticalUpdates.source: ESPN, BeIN Sports squad coverage - 15 May 2026

tacticalUpdates.oldValue: Sébastien Haller's aerial presence and finishing; Nicolas Pépé's direct dribbling and goal threat; Kessié's box-to-box power and late runs; AFCON-winning confidence and momentum
tacticalUpdates.newValue: Amad Diallo's speed and creativity; Bonny's Serie A form and movement at Inter; Adingra's directness from the wing for Monaco; Pepe still a threat off the bench; Kessie's box-to-box power and late runs
tacticalUpdates.source: ESPN, BeIN Sports squad coverage - 15 May 2026

tacticalUpdates.oldValue: Haller as focal striker with Kessie arriving from deep
tacticalUpdates.newValue: Rotational front line without a fixed No.9 after Haller's omission; Bonny, Wahi and Guessand expected to compete for the central role with Diallo and Adingra providing wide pace

squadChanges.details: AFCON 2024 final scorer left out of the 26-man squad named on 15 May 2026 after a season of limited minutes at FC Utrecht. Named only on the five-man reserve list, so could still be recalled if a forward withdraws.

Inter Milan forward, previously a France youth international, switched eligibility (approved 8 May 2026) and was named in the senior 26-man squad despite being uncapped. Direct beneficiary of Haller's omission.
squadChanges.source: ESPN, BeIN Sports, World Soccer Talk - May 2026

squadChanges.details: OGC Nice forward initially considered unlikely; included in the 26-man squad after Fae reassessed his form following a March international debut.
Veteran forward not included in the 26-man squad named on 15 May 2026.
Nice winger omitted from the 26-man squad on 15 May 2026.
Midfielder who had returned to the squad for qualifiers omitted from the final 26.
Striker option left out of the final 26-man squad.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: Squawka outright markets aggregator (citing US sportsbooks including bet365), May 2026; group winner odds via FOX Sports / RotoWire
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Outright winner around +30000 (roughly 0.33% implied). Group E winner odds around +800 (Germany favored at -300). Explicit 'to qualify from group' moneylines were not available in aggregator coverage at scout time; market consensus implies roughly a coin-flip for a top-two finish, with third place plus best-third progression a realistic backstop.

coachUpdate.notes: No change. Fae personally unveiled the 26-man squad at Stade Felix Houphouet-Boigny on 15 May 2026.

summary: Cote d'Ivoire's headline development is the 26-man squad announced by Emerse Fae on 15 May 2026: AFCON 2024 hero Sebastien Haller was dropped to the reserve list, while uncapped Inter Milan striker Ange-Yoan Bonny was added after a France-to-Ivory-Coast eligibility switch (approved 8 May). Captain Franck Kessie was included despite a late-April muscle scare at Al Ahli, and Crystal Palace forward Evann Guessand was selected despite not playing since 9 April with a knee injury. The only fixture announced for the new window is a marquee 4 June friendly against France in Nantes; no new internationals have been played since 16 April.
